2603.23301.title: Steering LLMs for Culturally Localized Generation
2603.23301.paper_summary: LLMs are deployed globally, yet produce responses biased towards cultures with abundant training data. Existing cultural localization approaches such as prompting or post-training alignment are black-box, hard to control, and do not reveal whether failures reflect missing knowledge or poor elicitation. In this paper, we address these gaps using mechanistic interpretability to uncover and manipulate cultural representations in LLMs. Leveraging sparse autoencoders, we identify interpretable features that encode culturally salient information and aggregate them into Cultural Embeddings (CuE). We use CuE both to analyze implicit cultural biases under underspecified prompts and to construct white-box steering interventions. Across multiple models, we show that CuE-based steering increases cultural faithfulness and elicits significantly rarer, long-tail cultural concepts than prompting alone. Notably, CuE-based steering is complementary to black-box localization methods, offering gains when applied on top of prompt-augmented inputs. This also suggests that models do benefit from better elicitation strategies, and don't necessarily lack long-tail knowledge representation, though this varies across cultures. Our results provide both diagnostic insight into cultural representations in LLMs and a controllable method to steer towards desired cultures.
2603.23301.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 在全球範圍內被部署，但其產生的回應往往偏向於擁有豐富訓練數據的文化。現有的文化本地化方法，如提示或後訓練對齊，都是黑箱的，難以控制，且無法揭示失敗是否反映了缺失的知識或不良的引導。在本文中，我們使用機械解釋性來解決這些空白，以發掘和操控 LLM 中的文化表徵。我們利用稀疏自編碼器，識別出編碼文化重要信息的可解釋特徵，並將其聚合成文化嵌入（CuE）。我們使用 CuE 來分析在不明確提示下的隱性文化偏見，並構建白箱引導干預。在多個模型中，我們顯示基於 CuE 的引導提高了文化忠實度，並引發了顯著更稀有的長尾文化概念，這比單純的提示更有效。值得注意的是，基於 CuE 的引導與黑箱本地化方法是互補的，當應用於增強提示的輸入時，提供了增益。這也表明模型確實受益於更好的引導策略，並不一定缺乏長尾知識表徵，儘管這在不同文化間有所變化。我們的結果提供了對 LLM 中文化表徵的診斷性洞察，以及一種可控的方法來引導至期望的文化。
2603.23301.author: Simran Khanuja et.al.
2603.23301.authors: Simran Khanuja, Hongbin Liu, Shujian Zhang, John Lambert, Mingqing Chen, Rajiv Mathews, Lun Wang
